content.problemStatement:
You are given a string SSS that contains of the characters AAA, BBB or CCC only.

In one operation, you can do either of the following:


Choose an occurrence of AB\text{AB}AB in the string, convert it to BA\text{BA}BA.
Choose an occurrence of BC\text{BC}BC in the string, convert it to CB\text{CB}CB.
Note that you are only allowed to operate on adjacent elements.

Find the maximum number of operations you can do. It can be proven the answer is finite.


Input Format

The first line of input will contain a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases.
Each test case consists of multiple lines of input.

The first line of each test case contains NNN - the length of the string.
The second line contains SSS - the string.




Output Format
For each test case, output on a new line the maximum number of operations possible.
constraints: The sum of NNN over all test cases does not exceed 2‚čÖ1052 \cdot 10^52‚čÖ105.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Test Case 1 : We can convert ABCC\text{ABCC}ABCC to ACBC\text{ACBC}ACBC and then to ACCB\text{ACCB}ACCB. More than 222 operations can be proven to be impossible.
